topic: A neighbor asks to use part of the yard for a garden
raw_output:
[story 1]

The afternoon sun beat down, turning the pavement into a shimmering mirage. I was leaning against the porch railing, my eyes half-closed, just trying to conserve the little energy I had left for the evening. Then, a tentative knock. It was Mr. Henderson, our neighbor, looking earnest, clutching a rolled-up blueprint. "Excuse me," he began, his voice pitched a little too high, "I was wondering if I might trouble you for a little bit of your yard? I have an idea for a raised bed, just a small section." I blinked slowly, the effort of focusing feeling monumental. My shoulders slumped further as I surveyed the expanse of lawn, already feeling the familiar weight settle in my chest.

[story 2]

I was trying to finish a book, but the words swam before my eyes. Every muscle in my body felt heavy, like wet sand. The sound of the doorbell startled me; it was Sarah from down the street. "Hi," I managed, my voice sounding rusty. She launched into her request for a shared patch of earth, describing intricate plans for herbs and flowers. I looked past her, toward the overgrown corner of my property, and a deep sigh escaped me, a sound more like air escaping a punctured tire. I just nodded, unable to muster the enthusiasm required to even look properly at the proposal.

[story 3]

The dust motes danced in the slivers of light cutting through the blinds. I hadn't changed out of my old t-shirt in hours, and the simple act of standing felt like lifting a boulder. When Mrs. Gable approached, her smile wide and hopeful, asking permission to use a strip of my garden for her vegetables, I just stared at the ground. My gaze drifted over the patchy soil, seeing only the endless maintenance ahead. "Oh," I mumbled, the sound barely audible above the distant traffic. I didn't meet her eyes, just pointed vaguely toward the fence line, a silent communication that said, 'I don't have the capacity for this right now.'
